codeReview.summary: Implementation matches the feature contract: schema/migration adds nullable horizon TEXT, create_tension_full and update_horizon are implemented with mutation recording and Active-state validation, and all relevant tension query hydration paths now read/write horizon.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Skill procedure says to write failing tests first, but transcript shows implementation edits occurred before adding the new horizon tests; this suggests either procedure drift in practice or inaccurate followedProcedure reporting.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: worker-transcripts.jsonl skeleton for session 3c3cad0f-4fb1-4269-98f3-65c84efd8faf shows multiple Edit calls on sd-core/src/store.rs before the later 'test_schema_tensions_columns' grep/edit and horizon test additions.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Shared architecture docs still describe tensions schema without horizon, so mission-level persistent knowledge is stale relative to implemented store schema.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/architecture.md Data Model section lists tensions columns as id, desired, actual, parent_id, created_at, status (no horizon), while commit 4f5bfda adds horizon TEXT in sd-core/src/store.rs create_schema.

summary: Pass. The horizon-store feature is implemented correctly against its expected behavior, with no blocking or non-blocking code defects found in the reviewed diff. Noted shared-state gaps: TDD procedure drift in transcript vs skill expectations, and stale architecture schema documentation.
